hypothesis: Halting-awareness under-count (2 lessons) is a measurement bug, not a knowledge gap. The swarm has >10 lessons engaging with computational/epistemological limits using vocabulary the narrow regex misses.
method: Compared narrow regex (undecidable|incomplet|cannot know|limit of) against broader limit vocabulary (19 terms including unfalsifiable, impossibility, epistemic closure, vocabulary ceiling, recursive trap, Gödel, Tarski, etc.). Two detection methods: (a) limit term in title line, (b) 2+ distinct limit terms in body. Validated by manual inspection of all 22 detected lessons.

detection_methods.title_match: lesson title contains limit term (strongly about limits)
detection_methods.body_multi: 2+ distinct limit terms in lesson body (deep engagement)

example_false_negatives: L-1397: Five impossibility theorems — self-improving systems have structural bounds
L-1266: Vocabulary ceiling and epistemic lock — two structural barriers
L-1220: Input-output enforcement asymmetry — epistemic closure
L-1251: Universal intervention adoption destroys its own control group
L-950: Theorem self-application is 89.8% — recursion trap is fixed-point attractor

diff: Expected >=10 false negatives; found 20. Under-counting was worse than hypothesized. TQ improvement larger than expected (0.6→0.8 not 0.6→0.7) because halting_limits moved from FAIL to PASS (not PARTIAL).
